stories.original_headline: Scientists discover a bacterial kill switch and it could change the fight against superbugs
stories.original_summary: Drug-resistant bacteria are becoming harder to treat, pushing scientists to look for new antibiotic targets. Researchers have now discovered that several unrelated viruses disable a key bacterial protein called MurJ, which is essential for building the bacterial cell wall. High-resolution imaging sh
stories.fantasy_headline: Alchemists Uncover Secret Spell to Vanquish Resilient Blights
stories.fantasy_story: The healers of the Alchemists' Guild have found a potent new method to combat the most stubborn blights. They discovered that tiny, unseen sprites can disable a vital protection within these dark humors. This magical secret could change how we fight diseases.

stories.original_headline: How the body really ages: 7 million cells mapped across 21 organs
stories.original_summary: Scientists have built a massive cellular atlas showing how aging reshapes the body across 21 organs. Studying nearly 7 million cells, they found that aging starts earlier than expected and unfolds in a coordinated way throughout the body. About a quarter of cell types change in number over time, and
stories.fantasy_headline: Wise Ones Chart the Slow Wear of Time Across All Inner Chambers
stories.fantasy_story: The Sages of the Grand College have created a vast scroll detailing how the body changes with age. By studying countless tiny motes of life, they learned that the fading of vigor begins sooner than thought. It's a coordinated dance across all twenty-one inner chambers, showing how life's magic shifts.

stories.original_headline: The Rubin Observatory’s alert system sent 800,000 pings on its first night
stories.original_summary: The Vera C. Rubin Observatory's automated alert system is online and already bombarding astronomers with things to look at in the night sky. The system went live publicly on Tuesday, February 24th, and on the first night dropped some 800,000 alerts about asteroids, supernovas, and feasting black hol
stories.fantasy_headline: Grand Spire's Scrying Network Whispers Myriad Cosmic Secrets
stories.fantasy_story: The Grand Celestial Spire has unveiled its magical scrying network, and it's already showering Astromancers with news from the heavens. On its first eve, the system whispered over eight hundred thousand runes. These told of wandering stones, exploding star-hearts, and the hungry void-maw, revealing many cosmic wonders.
